schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: The Form, embodied — the base-most layer of the whole spine, composing no one and composed by everyone. Four files under one charter: (1) core_values.py — the frozen CP1-CP6 contract (`CoreValue`, `CORE_VALUES`, `CoreValuesMixin`); (2) device.py — `BaseDevice`, which composes the mixin and declares the Form v0 #2 introspection surface (intention -> state -> settings -> other, assembled in order by concrete `introspect()`); (3) shim.py — `BaseShim`, which composes the mixin, pins `device_id`, and carries the per-pulse CALLBACK-FIRING: on each ground_loop heartbeat pulse, a device's shim evaluates its device's callbacks (each trigger where its data is owned, Law 6) and POKES the target of every one that fires, onto the bus — it also receives the device's mail and STARTS the device (its own process) on demand; (4) callback.py — the `Callback` primitive: an immutable 'poke X when this trigger is true', where a trigger is ANY predicate (not a named kind), the anti-reification made structural. Because both bases mix in `CoreValuesMixin`, every device and every shim carries the six structurally — you cannot be a device or a shim without them; and because callback-firing lives in the shim base, 'hang your callbacks on the heartbeat' is built into every device by inheritance, not re-implemented per device. The Form v0 #2 surface now has its first RENDER-side consumer built into the base pair (web-server ticket, child a): `BaseDevice.declared_panes()` lets a device OFFER extra panes beyond the STATUS+SETTINGS floor (each a handler returning DATA), and `BaseShim.active_page()` is the standard assembly — it projects the floor from `introspect()`, appends the declared panes in order (multiples of a kind allowed), renders an unwired or raising handler ABSENT-with-reason (CP2/Law 7), and returns pure DATA the web server renders. The machinery lives once in the base (Law 6), not per device. (5) diagnostic.py — `DiagnosticBase`, composed by `BaseDevice` so every device inherits `emit()` the way it inherits CP1-CP6: the transition-grade diagnostic surface Cairn lacked (outcome-grade VALIDATIONs/journeys said what PASSED; nothing spoke when system_rackmount went red SILENTLY). At a gate contact a device sends a THIN breadcrumb home to CC's shim (the diagnostic mailbox, for now — `BaseShim.receive_diagnostic`/`diagnostics()`), pointing to the ticket (`pointer`, the tie) and stamped to the microsecond (which indexes the fuller 30-day cache-class logs). The gate issues it — not the ticket; the emission stays dumb so the interpreter that crawls it can be smart (Law 1). Only the µs-stamp idea is carried from UU's `DiagnosticBase`; the loguru/JSON-sink backend is not.
why: Law 2 — CP1-CP6 hold everywhere, including in the process that builds the system. A value enforced by prose is policy; a value carried by a class you cannot subclass without is physics (Law 4). Form v0 #2 lives here for the same reason: a uniform introspection surface on the base means every device is observable by construction, not by each device remembering to be — the tester probes one protocol, the web UI renders one protocol. Placing the values here is necessary but not sufficient: a value is *consumed* only when a per-device contract turns it into a check (CP1 -> no 'done' without an honesty gate). This layer guarantees structural presence and the shape of the introspection surface; consumption is per-device charter work.
invoke: import: `from cairn.base.core_values import CoreValuesMixin, CORE_VALUES`; `from cairn.base.device import BaseDevice`; `from cairn.base.shim import BaseShim`; `from cairn.base.callback import Callback`; `from cairn.base.diagnostic import DiagnosticBase`. Prove: `python3 cairn/base/proofs/test_core_values.py`, `test_composition.py`, `test_callback.py`, `test_shim.py`, `test_diagnostic.py` (each exit 0 = green).
entry: MIXED. core_values.py entered by GRAFT (Law 8) — carried-over-intact from the quarry `UnseenUniversity/.../diagnostic_base/core_values.py` with its pin test; the core values are the one part of the predecessor never the problem (Law 2), ratified by Akien 2026-07-14. device.py + shim.py did NOT graft — UU's `BaseDevice`/`BaseShim`/`DiagnosticBase` (~1000 lines: 14 rack methods, block/halt state model, loguru JSON sinks, dispatch/policy/circuit machinery) are the design Cairn redesigned around. They carry the `CoreValuesMixin` composition and its teeth across, and — added 2026-07-22 — the microsecond-stamp idea into a Cairn-native `DiagnosticBase` (authored fresh: gate-issued thin breadcrumbs home to CC's shim, not loguru sinks); otherwise authored fresh to Cairn's Form v0. Recorded as a redesign, not a graft, so no one mistakes them for carried-over-intact.
state: PROVED for what exists. LANDED 2026-07-22 (the diagnostic-logging method, step 1 — logging): `DiagnosticBase` (diagnostic.py) composed by `BaseDevice`, so every device inherits `emit()`; a gate sends a thin breadcrumb (pointer to the ticket, µs-stamped) home to CC's shim (`BaseShim.receive_diagnostic`/`diagnostics()`), held-loud-not-lost when homeless (Law 7). Motivated by the system_rackmount SILENT red — nothing was watching the chain. PROVEN: test_diagnostic.py (8 teeth) green bare and under the tester, VALIDATION beside-code at cairn/base/validations/test_diagnostic.json; the full base suite + every device proof stayed green under the composition change. FILED EDGES: the 30-day cache-class json log tier in instance-space the µs stamp indexes (v0 mailbox is in-memory); the log INTERPRETER that crawls the mailbox+logs into a coherent slice (the method's step 2, next build); wiring emit's receiver to CC's real running shim (instance-space). CLOSED an earlier session (the open edge last session filed): the composition teeth — 'no device or shim can lack the values' — now that BaseDevice/BaseShim exist, proofs/test_composition.py proves it and BITES (verified red on a rogue device with empty values and a rogue shim missing CP6). RATIFIED 2026-07-17 by Akien (both design calls, previously CC's calls flagged for veto): (1) BaseDevice = Form v0's three ordered reports, NOT UU's 14 rack methods / block-halt — ratified as cast; (2) BaseShim's lifecycle + one-loop deferral — ratified, with the governing principle 'shim first, we keep adding things as we go along, and that's ok': the shim grows by accretion as a real need appears, it is not pre-built to UU's full contract (nothing-is-terminal; [[cc-reify-vs-flow]]). OPEN EDGES, filed not faked: (a) ADVANCING — the Form v0 #2 report *shape* is firming up now that its render-side consumer exists: `BaseShim.active_page()` (web-server child a, PROVEN 2026-07-22, test_active_page.py green under the tester, VALIDATION beside-code) projects intention/state/settings into the STATUS+SETTINGS pane floor + declared panes, fixing the shape the web UI renders. Still loose where no consumer pulls it (the tester's probe side, `other`'s internal shape); it tightens further as the web_server device (child b) and real devices declare panes; (b) BaseShim's one-loop primitive is now RESOLVED AND BUILT (2026-07-18, PROVEN — test_shim.py green): the shim's loop IS the per-pulse CALLBACK-firing driven by the ground_loop heartbeat. On each pulse, `on_pulse(now, context)` evaluates the device's callbacks (a batch that a bad callback cannot abort — CP2/Law 7) and pokes the target of each that fires, onto the bus; `deliver(envelope)` receives the device's mail and starts the device on demand (`_start_device`). This is what makes 'hang callbacks on the heartbeat' universal, and it is what the deferred one-loop (MAP.md:327) was waiting to become — no UU-style loop thread, because the heartbeat is the one loop (the ground_loop only beats; the shim handles the pulse). The CALLBACK is the immutable declaration (callback.py, class-space); the TICKET (mutable workflow node, instance-space) is a DIFFERENT species — LEARNING is a state, a callback is the worker a LEARNING node sets — and the ticket state-machine still waits on the emit-chokepoint (`CairnCommons/tickets/state-machine-physics.json`). The lifecycle contract (start/stop/etc.) beyond `_start_device`, and each device its own OS PROCESS (vs today's in-process instantiation), remain filed edges.
gates: proof gate — the two pin tests in proofs/, runnable bare (python3) today; the tester device will own how proofs run when it lands. Durability gate (code-seam): not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) the code and the canonical commons record (`CairnCommons/intentions-other/core-values.md`) disagree on the set, order, or any narrative (per that record's own falsifier); (2) `CORE_VALUES` is not exactly [CP1..CP6] in order, or the frozen `CoreValue` becomes mutable; (3) `BaseDevice` or `BaseShim` stops composing `CoreValuesMixin`, or any imported subclass of them overrides `CORE_VALUES` to a divergent set; (4) `introspect()` stops reporting intention -> state -> settings in that order; (5) `BaseDevice` stops composing `DiagnosticBase` or `emit` stops being inherited (a device that cannot send diagnostics home breaks 'transition-grade surface everywhere'), or a homeless `emit` silently DROPS a record instead of holding it loud (Law 7 — the silent-red defect this exists to end).
how_it_learns: The values themselves do not learn autonomously — by design. They are a frozen, ratified contract (Law 2), the terminal floor the rest of the system stands on; nothing-is-terminal does not apply to the axioms themselves. Their horizon is human: the words change only when Akien re-ratifies them (see core-values.md horizon). The Form v0 surface *does* have a learning path, but not here: each device charter answers, for itself, which CP became which enforced check and whether that check earns its keep, and the report *shape* firms up from what its consumers (tester, web UI) actually need. The base only guarantees the six are present to be consumed and the introspection surface is ordered and uniform.
traces_to: Law 5 (intent + code + proofs share an address)
Law 8 (entry by graft / a proof a hollow build couldn't pass)
Law 3 (nothing known until measured — the diagnostic emission IS the measurement)
Law 7 (errors loud at diagnostic surfaces — the transition-grade surface that ends the silent red)
Law 1 (the emission stays dumb; the interpreter compiles the inference once by crawling it)
Form v0 #2 (the ordered introspection surface, MAP.md:157)
Telos 6.1 (CP1-6 pinned by tests, enforced in-process)
owner: Akien owns the words (ratification is his gate). CC owns the enforced form (the code + its pin tests) and gates writes to it.
proof: proofs/test_core_values.py — the six in order, the frozen record + lookup helper, and the whitespace-normalized cross-check against core-values.md (drift caught by physics). proofs/test_composition.py — BaseDevice/BaseShim compose the mixin and carry the six, plus a sweep over every imported subclass (armed trap; 0 subclasses today, stated out loud). proofs/test_callback.py — a trigger is any predicate (evaluated where its data is owned), the callback is immutable, a defect is refused at construction. proofs/test_shim.py — a pulse fires due callbacks onto the bus (holding the rest, batch-surviving a bad one), and the device is woken on demand. proofs/test_active_page.py — the web presentation surface's DATA machinery (web-server child a): the STATUS+SETTINGS floor is projected from introspect(), declared panes append in order (multiples allowed), an unwired or raising handler is loud+absent not fatal, and the whole page json-round-trips (DATA, not HTML). proofs/test_diagnostic.py — the diagnostic surface: the µs stamp is the 6th place after the decimal (none lost), the gate issues a thin breadcrumb pointing to the ticket, it lands home in the mailbox in order, one ticket's entries share the pointer and sort by stamp (the crawl), a homeless emit is held-not-lost (Law 7), emit is inherited structurally (Law 2), and the mailbox is DATA the interpreter can crawl. Green: run all six under `python3`.
provenance: core_values.py born 2026-07-17 as the founding stone of the code spine (first class-space code). device.py + shim.py born 2026-07-17 (second step): the base pair, closing the composition-teeth open edge and casting the Form v0 #2 surface. callback.py born 2026-07-18 (evening) and shim.py reworked the same night: the heartbeat+callback+bus rework moved the per-pulse firing INTO the shim (correcting the ground_loop-executor goof) and made the Callback its own primitive — a third base alongside device and shim. Opens the CP/diagnostic base spine step (MAP.md build order) toward the tester as first device.
